generatedSentence: The village held a ceremonial `kickoff` under the `monsoon` sky, its `market` bustling, a `preview` of the season’s `kits`. Our hero, known by `nicknames` like 'The Lancer' in his past life, now lived `lowkey` in `pyjama` pants, `denying` the urge to `upgrade` his `bandwidth`. He `dabbed` at a `cauliflower` patch, cultivating it with `perfection`, reflecting on `unwanted` `difficulties` and the `gratitude` for `geckos` that patrolled his `raspberries`. He still kept an `inventory` of old `vouchers`, a tangible link to a less complicated `winter`. But `jeopardy` arose from a `reversal` of fortune, `seventy` `lamps` suddenly illuminating the dread `whereabouts` of an `angry` `faujdar`. This `landlord`-like figure, a `skinhead` with an `occult` agenda, wanted our hero to `obey` a `wacky` `fanatic` with a `vengeful` `kickout` mandate. A `nun` he knew had `nicked` some old tech, now corrupted by `voltage`, and she refused to `testify`. He heard a `deckhand` had `bolted` from the `guard` detail, causing a `tantrum` amongst them, all `glorifying` a false `honesty`. The `humans` of the cult, their arms `inked` with strange symbols, engaged in `mocking` a `victim` they called 'the `lamb`' near a `roadblock`. His `brother`, a former `lancer` now ensnared, was `knuckled` into a `blazer` and `jammed` by an unknown `malady`, his `recollection` blurred like `pasta` sauce. He had `least` expected such a `superlative` display of `malicious` intent, a `cool` but dangerous `groove` of power. A `liftoff` signal flared for the `ninth` ritual, and he saw `other` cultists `kissing` a totem, a chilling `onboarding` process that felt like an `obituary` for the innocent. He had to take `initiative`, unwilling to `withdraw` and let the cult `measure` lives like `badminton` scores. With a quick `drink`, he prepared for the fight, observing the `flora` around a `walled` compound. A `jackal` howled in the distance, a `witch` began her incantations, and a `kangaroo` hopped into the `flyways` of a `condor` circling above. His `plucky` spirit, a `brotherhood` `liked` by few, spurred him on. He donned an old `jacket`, ready to challenge the `rogue` elements, hoping to `release` the `bucket` of secrets and free the innocent.
panelSentences: The village held a ceremonial `kickoff` under the `monsoon` sky, its `market` bustling, a `preview` of the season’s `kits`. Our hero, known by `nicknames` like 'The Lancer' in his past life, now lived `lowkey` in `pyjama` pants, `denying` the urge to `upgrade` his `bandwidth`. He `dabbed` at a `cauliflower` patch, cultivating it with `perfection`, reflecting on `unwanted` `difficulties` and the `gratitude` for `geckos` that patrolled his `raspberries`. He still kept an `inventory` of old `vouchers`, a tangible link to a less complicated `winter`.

But `jeopardy` arose from a `reversal` of fortune, `seventy` `lamps` suddenly illuminating the dread `whereabouts` of an `angry` `faujdar`. This `landlord`-like figure, a `skinhead` with an `occult` agenda, wanted our hero to `obey` a `wacky` `fanatic` with a `vengeful` `kickout` mandate. A `nun` he knew had `nicked` some old tech, now corrupted by `voltage`, and she refused to `testify`. He heard a `deckhand` had `bolted` from the `guard` detail, causing a `tantrum` amongst them, all `glorifying` a false `honesty`.

The `humans` of the cult, their arms `inked` with strange symbols, engaged in `mocking` a `victim` they called 'the `lamb`' near a `roadblock`. His `brother`, a former `lancer` now ensnared, was `knuckled` into a `blazer` and `jammed` by an unknown `malady`, his `recollection` blurred like `pasta` sauce. He had `least` expected such a `superlative` display of `malicious` intent, a `cool` but dangerous `groove` of power. A `liftoff` signal flared for the `ninth` ritual, and he saw `other` cultists `kissing` a totem, a chilling `onboarding` process that felt like an `obituary` for the innocent.

He had to take `initiative`, unwilling to `withdraw` and let the cult `measure` lives like `badminton` scores. With a quick `drink`, he prepared for the fight, observing the `flora` around a `walled` compound. A `jackal` howled in the distance, a `witch` began her incantations, and a `kangaroo` hopped into the `flyways` of a `condor` circling above. His `plucky` spirit, a `brotherhood` `liked` by few, spurred him on. He donned an old `jacket`, ready to challenge the `rogue` elements, hoping to `release` the `bucket` of secrets and free the innocent.
rawPanelSentences: The village held a ceremonial `kickoff` under the `monsoon` sky, its `market` bustling, a `preview` of the season’s `kits`. Our hero, known by `nicknames` like 'The Lancer' in his past life, now lived `lowkey` in `pyjama` pants, `denying` the urge to `upgrade` his `bandwidth`. He `dabbed` at a `cauliflower` patch, cultivating it with `perfection`, reflecting on `unwanted` `difficulties` and the `gratitude` for `geckos` that patrolled his `raspberries`. He still kept an `inventory` of old `vouchers`, a tangible link to a less complicated `winter`.

But `jeopardy` arose from a `reversal` of fortune, `seventy` `lamps` suddenly illuminating the dread `whereabouts` of an `angry` `faujdar`. This `landlord`-like figure, a `skinhead` with an `occult` agenda, wanted our hero to `obey` a `wacky` `fanatic` with a `vengeful` `kickout` mandate. A `nun` he knew had `nicked` some old tech, now corrupted by `voltage`, and she refused to `testify`. He heard a `deckhand` had `bolted` from the `guard` detail, causing a `tantrum` amongst them, all `glorifying` a false `honesty`.

The `humans` of the cult, their arms `inked` with strange symbols, engaged in `mocking` a `victim` they called 'the `lamb`' near a `roadblock`. His `brother`, a former `lancer` now ensnared, was `knuckled` into a `blazer` and `jammed` by an unknown `malady`, his `recollection` blurred like `pasta` sauce. He had `least` expected such a `superlative` display of `malicious` intent, a `cool` but dangerous `groove` of power. A `liftoff` signal flared for the `ninth` ritual, and he saw `other` cultists `kissing` a totem, a chilling `onboarding` process that felt like an `obituary` for the innocent.

He had to take `initiative`, unwilling to `withdraw` and let the cult `measure` lives like `badminton` scores. With a quick `drink`, he prepared for the fight, observing the `flora` around a `walled` compound. A `jackal` howled in the distance, a `witch` began her incantations, and a `kangaroo` hopped into the `flyways` of a `condor` circling above. His `plucky` spirit, a `brotherhood` `liked` by few, spurred him on. He donned an old `jacket`, ready to challenge the `rogue` elements, hoping to `release` the `bucket` of secrets and free the innocent.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. The protagonist is a cyber-lancer, wearing a sleek obsidian-black combat blazer with glowing neon blue circuitry patterns, a single red ocular lens integrated into his high-tech helmet. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The cyber-lancer, in casual pyjama pants, tending a glowing cauliflower patch, a small gecko on his shoulder, looking up at a stormy monsoon sky, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The cyber-lancer, now armored in his combat blazer, confronts an elderly, angry 'faujdar' in a hovering vehicle, multiple glowing lamps casting long shadows, medium close-up, dynamic angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The cyber-lancer, hidden behind ancient flora, observes a group of heavily inked cultists mocking a kneeling figure near a glowing energy roadblock, low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The cyber-lancer in a dynamic pose, releasing a high-tech grappling hook towards a walled compound, a large condor soaring in the background, wide action shot.

definitions.definition: A rapid turn performed by shifting one's weight to the rear of the surfboard.
The opening kick of each half of a game of football.
(by extension) the opening sequence of any event
Brassica oleracea var. botrytis, an annual variety of cabbage, of which the cluster of young flower stalks and buds is eaten as a vegetable.
The edible head or curd of a cauliflower plant.
The swelling of a cauliflower ear.
A cavalry soldier armed with a lance weapon
Displaying or feeling anger.
definitions.example: An angry mob started looting the warehouse.

definitions.definition: (said about a wound or a rash) Inflamed and painful.
definitions.example: The broken glass left two angry cuts across my arm.

definitions.definition: (said about the elements, like the sky or the sea) Dark and stormy, menacing.
definitions.example: Angry clouds raced across the sky.

definitions.definition: A gathering of people for the purchase and sale of merchandise at a set time, often periodic.
definitions.example: The privilege to hold a weekly market was invaluable for any feudal era burgh.

definitions.definition: City square or other fairly spacious site where traders set up stalls and buyers browse the merchandise.
A grocery store
definitions.example: Stop by the market on your way home and pick up some milk

definitions.definition: A group of potential customers for one's product.
definitions.example: We believe that the market for the new widget is the older homeowner.

definitions.definition: A geographical area where a certain commercial demand exists.
definitions.example: Foreign markets were lost as our currency rose versus their valuta.

definitions.definition: A formally organized, sometimes monopolistic, system of trading in specified goods or effects.
definitions.example: The stock market ceased to be monopolized by the paper-shuffling national stock exchanges with the advent of Internet markets.

definitions.definition: The sum total traded in a process of individuals trading for certain commodities.
The price for which a thing is sold in a market; hence, value; worth.
To make (products or services) available for sale and promote them.
definitions.example: We plan to market an ecology model by next quarter.

definitions.definition: To sell
definitions.example: We marketed more this quarter already than all last year!

definitions.definition: To deal in a market; to buy or sell; to make bargains for provisions or goods.
An experience of something in advance.
An advance showing of a film, exhibition etc.
Something seen in advance.
A facility for seeing and checking a document, or changes to it, before saving and/or printing it.
To show or watch something, or part of it, before it is complete.
Clothes for wearing to bed and sleeping in, usually consisting of a loose-fitting shirt and pants/trousers.
Loose-fitting trousers worn by both sexes in various southern Asian countries including India.
A familiar, invented given name for a person or thing used instead of the actual name of the person or thing.
A kind of byname that describes a person by a characteristic of that person.
Danger of loss, harm, or failure.
definitions.example: The poor condition of the vehicle put its occupants in constant jeopardy.

definitions.definition: To jeopardize; to endanger.
A piece of paper that entitles the holder to a discount, or that can be exchanged for goods and services.
A receipt.
One who or that which vouches.
A copy of a published advertisement sent by the agency to the client as proof of publication.
A mechanical device used in shops for automatically registering the amount of money drawn.
To establish the authenticity of; to vouch for.
To provide a vouch for (an expenditure).
To provide (a beneficiary) with a voucher.
An officer tasked with the administration of a sarkar.
A circular wooden vessel, made of hooped staves.
A kind of basket made especially from straw of rushes, especially for holding fish; by extension, the contents of such a basket or similar container, used as a measure of weight.
definitions.example: 1961 18 Jan, Guardian (cited after OED):

definitions.definition: A collection of items forming the equipment of a soldier, carried in a knapsack.
Any collection of items needed for a specific purpose, especially for use by a workman, or personal effects packed for travelling.
definitions.example: Always carry a good first-aid kit.

definitions.definition: A collection of parts sold for the buyer to assemble.
definitions.example: I built the entire car from a kit.

definitions.definition: The set of skills and abilities chosen for a playable character.
The standard set of clothing, accessories and equipment worn by players.
Clothing.
definitions.example: Get your kit off and come to bed.

definitions.definition: A full software distribution, as opposed to a patch or upgrade.
A drum kit.
To assemble or collect something into kits or sets or to give somebody a kit. See also kit out and other derived phrases.
definitions.example: We need to kit the parts for the assembly by Friday, so that manufacturing can build the tool.

definitions.definition: A kitten (young cat).
A kit fox.
A young skunk.
A school of pigeons, especially domesticated, trained pigeons.

A very small long-necked violin that came in a variety of shapes and configurations, meant to be carried in a pocket and intended for instructors (dancing masters) to carry to accompany their students.
The state of being reversed.
An instance of reversing.
A change in fortune; a change from being successful to having problems.
Intended to reverse; implying reversal.
The cardinal number occurring after sixty-nine and before seventy-one, represented in Roman numerals as LXX and in Arabic numerals as 70.
A device that generates heat, light or other radiation. Especially an electric light bulb.
A device containing oil, burnt through a wick for illumination; an oil lamp.
A piece of furniture holding one or more electric light sockets.
To hit, clout, belt, wallop.
To hunt at night using a lamp; see lamping.
To hang out or chill; to do nothing in particular.
To do as ordered by (a person, institution etc), to act according to the bidding of.
To do as one is told.
To be obedient, compliant (to a given law, restriction etc.).
(usually with "the") Supernatural affairs.
To cover or hide from view.
definitions.example: The Earth occults the Moon during a lunar eclipse.

definitions.definition: To dissimulate, conceal, or obfuscate.
Secret; hidden from general knowledge; undetected.
definitions.example: occult blood loss;  occult cancer

definitions.definition: Related to the occult; pertaining to mysticism, magic, or astrology.
Esoteric.
The act of hovering
To float in the air.
definitions.example: The hummingbird hovered by the plant.

definitions.definition: To linger or hang in one place, especially in an uncertain manner.
definitions.example: His pen hovered above the paper.

definitions.definition: To waver, or be uncertain.
definitions.example: Filling in the voting form, I hovered between Labour and Liberal Democrat.

definitions.definition: To place the cursor over a hyperlink or icon without clicking.
definitions.example: A tooltip appears when you hover over this link.

definitions.definition: Zany; eccentric

Traditionally the fourth of the four seasons, typically regarded as being from December 23 to March 20 in continental regions of the Northern Hemisphere or the months of June, July and August in the Southern Hemisphere. It is the time when the sun is lowest in the sky, resulting in short days, and the time of year with the lowest atmospheric temperatures for the region.
The period of decay, old age, death, or the like.
Someone with dark skin, eyes and hair, seen as best suited to certain colors of clothing.
An appliance to be fixed on the front of a grate, to keep a kettle warm, etc.
To spend the winter (in a particular place).
definitions.example: When they retired, they hoped to winter in Florida.

definitions.definition: To store something (for instance animals) somewhere over winter to protect it from cold.
To disallow or reject.
definitions.example: I wanted to go to the party, but I was denied.

definitions.definition: To assert that something is not true.
definitions.example: Everyone knows he committed the crime, but he still denies it.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To refuse to give or grant something to someone.
definitions.example: My father denied me a good education.

definitions.definition: To take something away from someone; to deprive of.
To prevent from scoring.
To disclaim connection with, responsibility for, etc.; to refuse to acknowledge; to disown; to abjure; to disavow.
To refuse (to do or accept something).
The act of one who denies something; a denial.
To press lightly in a repetitive motion with a soft object without rubbing.
definitions.example: I dabbed my face with a towel.

definitions.definition: To apply a substance in this way.
definitions.example: He dabbed moisturizing liquid on his face.

definitions.definition: To strike by a thrust; to hit with a sudden blow or thrust.
To apply hash oil to a heated surface for the purpose of efficient combustion.
To perform the dab dance move, by moving both arms to one side of the body parallel with your head.
The width, usually measured in hertz, of a frequency band.
Of a signal, the width of the smallest frequency band within which the signal can fit.
The rate of data flow in digital networks typically measured in bits per second; the bitrate.
The capacity, energy or time required.
definitions.example: I think it's a worthy project, but I just don't have the bandwidth right now.

definitions.definition: Any of a number of winds associated with regions where most rain falls during a particular season.
Tropical rainy season when the rain lasts for several months with few interruptions.
The rains themselves.
Entire meteorological systems with such characteristics.
A member of a Christian religious community of women who live by certain vows and usually wear a habit, (specifically) those living together in a cloister.
(by extension) A member of a similar female community in other confessions.
A prostitute.
A kind of pigeon with the feathers on its head like the hood of a nun.
To make a nick or notch in; to cut or scratch in a minor way.
definitions.example: I nicked myself while I was shaving.

definitions.definition: To fit into or suit, as by a correspondence of nicks; to tally with.
To make a cut at the side of the face.
To steal.
definitions.example: Someone’s nicked my bike!

definitions.definition: (law enforcement) To arrest.
definitions.example: The police nicked him climbing over the fence of the house he’d broken into.

definitions.definition: To give or call (someone) by a nickname; to style.
Having one or more nicks; notched.
Stolen.
Captured (by police); arrested.
An upward grade or slope.
An improved component or replacement item, usually applied to technology
definitions.example: With my phone company, I get a free upgrade every twelve months if I keep topping up 10 pounds a month.

definitions.definition: An improvement
definitions.example: The remarried couples among my friends often joked that their divorce and remarriage amounted to an upgrade.

definitions.definition: To improve, usually applied to technology, generally by complete replacement of one or more components
definitions.example: When you upgrade your standard of living, you become addicted to that standard of living.

definitions.definition: To replace with something better.
definitions.example: I had to upgrade my anti-virus software to protect my computer from the newer threats.

definitions.definition: To improve the equipment or furnishings of or services rendered to
definitions.example: They upgraded him to the latest model.

definitions.definition: To improve in condition or status.
definitions.example: She upgraded to a more successful husband.

definitions.definition: To replace a program with a later version of itself, a version having a higher version number or marketed under a more recent product name.
Up a slope or grade.
To make a declaration, or give evidence, under oath.
To make a statement based on personal knowledge or faith.
An often childish display or fit of bad temper.
definitions.example: Baby Shawn threw a tantrum when he was told the bicycle was not his.

definitions.definition: To throw a tantrum.
A plait or fold in cloth, made by drawing a thread through it; a pucker.
The inclination forward of the axle journals to keep the wheels from working outward.
The soffit or under surface of the masonry required in gathering. See gather (transitive verb).
A blob of molten glass collected on the end of a blowpipe.
A gathering.
To collect; normally separate things.
definitions.example: I've been gathering ideas from the people I work with.

definitions.definition: To bring parts of a whole closer.
definitions.example: She gathered the shawl about her as she stepped into the cold.

definitions.definition: To infer or conclude; to know from a different source.
definitions.example: From his silence, I gathered that things had not gone well.

definitions.definition: (of a boil or sore) To be filled with pus
definitions.example: Salt water can help boils to gather and then burst.

definitions.definition: To collect molten glass on the end of a tool.
To gain; to win.
The difference in electrostatic potential between two points in space, especially between live and neutral conductors or the earth.
definitions.example: Be careful when opening high-voltage equipment.

definitions.definition: Location; where something is situated.
definitions.example: The whereabouts of the escaped snake are unknown.

definitions.definition: In, at or near what location
definitions.example: Whereabouts do you live?

definitions.definition: A member of the crew of a merchant ship who performs manual labour.
To work on a boat as a deckhand; crew.
Someone with a shaved head.
Member of the skinhead subculture arising in late 1960s England or its diaspora, often incorrectly associated with violence and white-supremacist or anti-immigrant principles.
To connect or assemble pieces using a bolt.
definitions.example: Bolt the vice to the bench.

definitions.definition: To secure a door by locking or barring it.
definitions.example: Bolt the door.

definitions.definition: To flee, to depart, to accelerate suddenly.
definitions.example: Seeing the snake, the horse bolted.

definitions.definition: To cause to start or spring forth; to dislodge (an animal being hunted).
definitions.example: to bolt a rabbit

definitions.definition: To strike or fall suddenly like a bolt.
To escape.
Of a plant, to grow quickly; to go to seed.
definitions.example: Lettuce and spinach will bolt as the weather warms up.

definitions.definition: To swallow food without chewing it.
To drink one's drink very quickly; to down a drink.
definitions.example: Come on, everyone, bolt your drinks; I want to go to the next pub!

definitions.definition: To refuse to support a nomination made by a party or caucus with which one has been connected; to break away from a party.
To utter precipitately; to blurt or throw out.
To sift, especially through a cloth.
To sift the bran and germ from wheat flour.
definitions.example: Graham flour is unbolted flour.

definitions.definition: To separate, assort, refine, or purify by other means.
To discuss or argue privately, and for practice, as cases at law.

A racquet sport played indoors on a court by two opposing players (singles) or two opposing pairs of players (doubles), in which a shuttlecock is volleyed over a net and the competitions are presided by an umpire in British English and a referee in American English.
A cooling summer drink made with claret, sugar and soda water.
A young sheep.
The flesh of a lamb or sheep used as food.
A person who is meek, docile and easily led.
A simple, unsophisticated person.
One who ignorantly speculates on the stock exchange and is victimized.
Of a sheep, to give birth.
To assist (sheep) to give birth.
definitions.example: The shepherd was up all night, lambing her young ewes.

definitions.definition: A person who owns and rents land such as a house, apartment, or condo.
The owner or manager of a public house.
(with "the") A shark, imagined as the owner of the surf to be avoided.
To pierce or puncture slightly.
definitions.example: John hardly felt the needle prick his arm when the adept nurse drew blood.

definitions.definition: To form by piercing or puncturing.
definitions.example: to prick a pattern for embroidery

definitions.definition: To mark or denote by a puncture; to designate by pricking; to choose; to mark.
To mark the surface of (something) with pricks or dots; especially, to trace a ship’s course on (a chart).
To run a middle seam through the cloth of a sail.
To fix by the point; to attach or hang by puncturing.
definitions.example: to prick a knife into a board

definitions.definition: To be punctured; to suffer or feel a sharp pain, as by puncture.
definitions.example: A sore finger pricks.

definitions.definition: To make or become sharp; to erect into a point; to raise, as something pointed; said especially of the ears of an animal, such as a horse or dog; and usually followed by up.
definitions.example: The dog's ears pricked up at the sound of a whistle.

definitions.definition: Usually in the form prick out: to plant (seeds or seedlings) in holes made in soil at regular intervals.
To incite, stimulate, goad.
To urge one's horse on; to ride quickly.
To affect with sharp pain; to sting, as with remorse.
To make acidic or pungent.
To become sharp or acid; to turn sour, as wine.
To aim at a point or mark.
Usually as prick up: to dress or adorn; to prink.
Punctured by small holes
To exalt, or give glory or praise to (something or someone).
To make (something) appear to be more glorious than it is; regard something or someone as excellent baselessly.
To worship or extol.
Vindictive or wanting vengeance.
definitions.example: His chains now broken, the prisoner turned a vengeful eye toward his former captors.

definitions.definition: The act, quality, or condition of being honest.
definitions.example: academic / artistic / emotional / intellectual honesty

definitions.definition: Honor; decency, propriety.
Chastity.
Any of various crucifers in the genus Lunaria, several of which are grown as ornamentals, particularly Lunaria annua.
A human being, whether man, woman or child.
definitions.example: Humans share common ancestors with other apes.

definitions.definition: Having a tattoo or tattoos.
A person who is zealously enthusiastic for some cause, especially in religion.
Fanatical.
Showing evidence of possession by a god or demon; frenzied, overzealous.
The act of recollecting, or recalling to the memory; the act of recalling to memory
The power of recalling ideas to the mind, or the period within which things can be recollected; remembrance
definitions.example: Alas that distant event isn't within my recollection anymore.

definitions.definition: That which is recollected; something called to mind; a reminiscence.
The act or practice of collecting or concentrating the mind; concentration; self-control.
definitions.example: From such an education Charles contracted habits of gravity and recollection.

definitions.definition: A spiritual retreat, especially one that is short.
Dark; characterised by dark tones and muted colours.
Restrained, subtle, not trying to attract attention.
definitions.example: She deserves an Oscar for her low-key performance in that movie.

definitions.definition: The quality or state of being perfect or complete, so that nothing substandard remains; the highest attainable state or degree of excellence
definitions.example: The system runs to perfection.

definitions.definition: A quality, endowment, or acquirement completely excellent; an ideal; faultlessness; especially, the divine attribute of complete excellence.
To perfect.
One who or that which is not wanted; an undesirable.
Not wanted; unwelcome.
The state of being grateful.
Any lizard of the family Gekkonidae. They are small, carnivorous, mostly nocturnal animals with large eyes and adhesive toes enabling them to climb on vertical and upside-down surfaces.
To move in the manner of a gecko; to attach to a vertical or upside-down surface.
(operations) The stock of an item on hand at a particular location or business.
definitions.example: Due to an undersized inventory at the Boston outlet, customers had to travel to Providence to find the item.

definitions.definition: (operations) A detailed list of all of the items on hand.
definitions.example: The inventory included several items that one wouldn't normally think to find at a cheese shop.

definitions.definition: (operations) The process of producing or updating such a list.
definitions.example: This month's inventory took nearly three days.

definitions.definition: A space containing the items available to a character for immediate use.
definitions.example: You can't get through the underground tunnel if there are more than three items in your inventory.

definitions.definition: (operations) To take stock of the resources or items on hand; to produce an inventory.
definitions.example: The main job of the night shift was to inventory the store, and restock when necessary.

definitions.definition: A series of frustrations
The state of being difficult, or hard to do.
An obstacle that hinders achievement of a goal.
definitions.example: We faced a difficulty in trying to book a flight so late.

definitions.definition: (sometimes in the plural) Physical danger from the environment, especially with risk of drowning
definitions.example: 2012 August 2, "Children rescued after getting into difficulties in Donegal" BBC Online

definitions.definition: An objection.
That which cannot be easily understood or believed.
An awkward situation or quarrel.
The plant Rubus idaeus.
Any of many other (but not all) species in the genus Rubus.
The juicy aggregate fruit of these plants.
A red colour, the colour of a ripe raspberry.
To gather or forage for raspberries.

A noise intended to imitate the passing of flatulence, made by blowing air out of the mouth while the tongue is protruding from and pressed against the lips, or by blowing air through the lips while they are pressed firmly together or against skin, used humorously or to express derision.
A cripple.
To make the noise intended to imitate the passing of flatulence.
A medium-sized rodent belonging to the genus Rattus.

Any of the numerous members of several rodent families (e.g. voles and mice) that resemble true rats in appearance, usually having a pointy snout, a long, bare tail, and body length greater than about 12 cm, or 5 inches.
A person who is known for betrayal; a scoundrel; a quisling.

definitions.definition: An informant or snitch.
A scab: a worker who acts against trade union policies.
A person who routinely spends time at a particular location.

definitions.definition: A wad of shed hair used as part of a hairstyle.
A roll of material used to puff out the hair, which is turned over it.
Vagina.
definitions.example: Get your rat out.

definitions.definition: Short for muskrat.
(usually with “on” or “out”) To betray a person or party, especially by telling their secret to an authority or an enemy; to turn someone in.
definitions.example: He is going to rat us out!

definitions.definition: To work as a scab, going against trade union policies.
(of a dog, etc.) To kill rats.
A scratch or a score.
A place in the sea with rapid currents and crags where a ship is likely to be torn apart in stormy weather.
To scratch or score.
definitions.example: He ratted a vertical line on his face with a pocket knife.

definitions.definition: To tear, rip, rend.
definitions.example: Ratted to shreds.

definitions.definition: Damn, drat, blast; used in oaths.
A ration.
Expression of annoyance or disgust; damn, darn.
Expression of disbelief.
To make a brief, basic drawing.
definitions.example: I usually sketch with a pen rather than a pencil.

definitions.definition: To describe briefly and with very few details.
definitions.example: He sketched the accident, sticking to the facts as they had happened.

definitions.definition: Either of two New World vultures, Vultur gryphus of the Andes or Gymnogyps californianus, a nearly extinct vulture of the mountains of California.
A gold coin of some South American countries bearing the figure of one of these vultures.
An Argentinian short range ballistic missile.
In golf, four under par (quadruple birdie, triple eagle, or double albatross)
Of, pertaining to, or as a result of malice or spite
Spiteful and deliberately harmful
definitions.example: He was sent off for a malicious tackle on Jones.

definitions.definition: An other, another (person, etc), more often rendered as another.
definitions.example: I'm afraid little Robbie does not always play well with others.

definitions.definition: The other one; the second of two.
definitions.example: One boat is not better than the other.

definitions.definition: To regard, label or treat as an "other", as not part of the same group; to view as different and alien.
To treat as different or separate; segregate; ostracise.
See other (determiner) below
Second.
definitions.example: I get paid every other week.

definitions.definition: Alien.
Different.
Left, as opposed to right.
Apart from; in the phrase "other than".
definitions.example: Other than that, I'm fine.

definitions.definition: Otherwise.
definitions.example: If you think other. — Shakespeare.

definitions.definition: The process of bringing a new employee on board, incorporating training and orientation.
A long, narrow channel or depression; e.g., such a slot cut into a hard material to provide a location for an engineering component, a tyre groove, or a geological channel or depression.
A fixed routine.
The middle of the strike zone in baseball where a pitch is most easily hit.
A pronounced, enjoyable rhythm.
A shaft or excavation.
A racing line, a path across the racing circuit's surface that a racecar will usually track on. (Note: There may be multiple grooves on any particular circuit or segment of circuit)
To cut a groove or channel in; to form into channels or grooves; to furrow.
To perform, dance to, or enjoy rhythmic music.
definitions.example: I was just starting to groove to the band when we had to leave.

definitions.definition: To strike for admittance; to rap upon, as a door.
To criticize verbally; to denigrate; to undervalue.

definitions.definition: To kick a ball towards another player; to pass.
To impress forcibly or strongly; to astonish; to move to admiration or applause.
To bump or impact.

definitions.definition: To rap one's knuckles against something, especially wood.

definitions.definition: To touch with the lips or press the lips against, usually to show love or affection or passion, or as part of a greeting.
To (cause to) touch lightly or slightly; to come into contact.
definitions.example: His ball kissed the black into the corner pocket.

definitions.definition: Of two or more people, to touch each other's lips together, usually to express love or affection or passion.
To treat with fondness.
That kisses.
Just touching.
The point in the launch of a rocket or an aircraft where it leaves contact with the ground.
The point at which a person or animal leaves the ground, as for example when jumping.
Any ailment or disease of the body; especially, a lingering or deep-seated disorder.
A moral or mental defect or disorder.
Dough made from wheat and water and sometimes mixed with egg and formed into various shapes; often sold in dried form and typically boiled for eating.
A dish or serving of pasta.
A type of pasta.
A brief notice of a person’s death, as published in a newspaper.
A biography of a recently deceased person, written by a journalist and published in a newspaper.
A register of deaths in a monastery.
Relating to the death of a person.
To get something stuck in a confined space.

definitions.definition: To brusquely force something into a space; cram, squeeze.
definitions.example: The rush-hour train was jammed with commuters.

definitions.definition: To cause congestion or blockage. Often used with "up"
definitions.example: A single accident can jam the roads for hours.

definitions.definition: To block or confuse a broadcast signal.
To throw a pitch at or near the batter's hands.

definitions.definition: To play music (especially improvisation as a group, or an informal unrehearsed session).
To injure a finger or toe by sudden compression of the digit's tip.

definitions.definition: To attempt to score points.
definitions.example: Toughie jammed four times in the second period.

definitions.definition: To bring (a vessel) so close to the wind that half her upper sails are laid aback.
To give up on a date or some joint endeavour; stand up, chicken out, jam out.
Stuck.

definitions.definition: Overcrowded; congested
definitions.example: A jammed Oktoberfest beer tent.

definitions.definition: A person who, or thing that, protects or watches over something.

definitions.definition: A garda; a police officer.
A squad responsible for protecting something.
definitions.example: The president inspected the guard of honour.

definitions.definition: The part of a sword that protects the wielder's hand.
A part of a machine which blocks access to dangerous parts.

definitions.definition: A watchchain.
A panel of a car that encloses the wheel area, especially the front wheels.
A state of caution; posture of defence.
Something worn to protect part of the body, e.g. the shins in cricket.
A relatively short player, playing farther from the basket than a forward or center.
The position on the popping crease where a batsman makes a mark to align himself with the wicket; see take guard.

Either of two offensive positions between the center and each of the offensive tackles, whose main responsibilities are to protect the quarterback, and open up "holes" through which offensive players can run.
A player playing a position named guard.
An employee, normally travelling in the last vehicle of a train, responsible for the safety of the train.
A Boolean expression that must evaluate to true for a branch of program execution to continue.
To protect from danger; to secure against surprise, attack, or injury; to keep in safety; to defend.
To keep watch over, in order to prevent escape or restrain from acts of violence, or the like.

definitions.definition: To watch by way of caution or defense; to be caution; to be in a state or position of defense or safety.

definitions.definition: To protect the edge of, especially with an ornamental border; hence, to face or ornament with lists, laces, etc.
To fasten by binding; to gird.
A flat pastry pressed with a grid pattern.

definitions.definition: A potato waffle, a savoury flat potato cake with the same kind of grid pattern.
To smash.
To apply pressure, or rub or massage with one's knuckles.

definitions.definition: To bend the fingers.
To touch one's forehead as a mark of respect.
To yield.
(chiefly in combination) Having a certain type of knuckles.
Something that blocks or obstructs a road.
An obstacle or impediment.
To prevent, hinder.
The highest extent or degree of something.

(grammar) The form of an adjective that expresses which of several items has the highest degree of the quality expressed by the adjective; in English, formed by appending "-est" to the end of the adjective (for some short adjectives only) or putting "most" before it.
definitions.example: The superlative of "big" is "biggest".

definitions.definition: An adjective used to praise something exceptional.

definitions.definition: Exceptionally good; of the highest quality; superb.
(grammar) Of or relating to a superlative.
A moderate or refreshing state of cold; moderate temperature of the air between hot and cold; coolness.

definitions.definition: A calm temperament.
The property of being cool, popular or in fashion.
Having a slightly low temperature; mildly or pleasantly cold.
Allowing or suggesting heat relief.

definitions.definition: Of a color, in the range of violet to green.
definitions.example: If you have a reddish complexion, you should mainly wear cool colors.

definitions.definition: Of a person, not showing emotion; calm and in control of oneself.
Unenthusiastic, lukewarm, skeptical.

definitions.definition: Calmly audacious.
definitions.example: In control as always, he came up with a cool plan.

definitions.definition: Applied facetiously to a sum of money, commonly as if to give emphasis to the largeness of the amount.
Of a person, knowing what to do and how to behave; considered popular by others.
In fashion, part of or fitting the in crowd; originally hipster slang.
Of an action, all right; acceptable; that does not present a problem.

definitions.definition: Of a person, not upset by circumstances that might ordinarily be upsetting.

definitions.definition: Quietly impudent, defiant, or selfish; deliberately presuming: said of persons and acts.
Son of the same parents as another person.
A male having at least one parent in common with another (see half-brother, stepbrother).
A male fellow member of a religious community, church, trades union etc.

definitions.definition: A form of address to a man.

definitions.definition: A black male.
Somebody, usually male, connected by a common cause or situation.
Someone who is a peer, whether male or female.
To treat as a brother.
Expressing exasperation.

definitions.definition: Something of the smallest possible extent; an indivisible unit.

definitions.definition: In the smallest or lowest degree; in a degree below all others.

definitions.definition: An abnormal blood-filled swelling of an artery or vein, resulting from a localized weakness in the wall of the vessel.
One that is harmed—killed, injured, subjected to oppression, deceived, or otherwise adversely affected—by someone or something, especially another person or event, force, or condition; in particular:

definitions.definition: A living being which is slain and offered as a sacrifice, usually in a religious rite.
A beginning; a first move.
A new development; a fresh approach to something; a new way of dealing with a problem.
The ability to act first or on one's own.

Serving to initiate; inceptive; initiatory; introductory; preliminary.
In which voter initiatives can be brought to the ballot.
To pull (something) back, aside, or away.
To stop talking to, or interacting with, other people and start thinking thoughts that are not related to what is happening around.

To retreat.

definitions.definition: To consume alcoholic beverages.

To smoke, as tobacco.
A semi-formal jacket.
A person or thing that blazes (marks or cuts a route).

To make fun of by mimicking, to taunt.
To tantalise, and disappoint the hopes of.

Derisive or contemptuous

Metrical rhythm.
A course of action.

definitions.definition: To judge, value, or appraise.
To obtain or set apart; to mark in even increments.
To traverse, cross, pass along; to travel over.
To adjust by a rule or standard.
To allot or distribute by measure; to set off or apart by measure; often with out or off.
The person or thing in the ninth position.
One of nine equal parts of a whole.

The compound interval between any tone and the tone represented on the ninth degree of the staff above it, as between one of the scale and two of the octave above; the octave of the second, consisting of 13 or 14 semitones (called minor and major ninth).
To lose a ninth
To divide by nine
The ordinal form of the number nine.
Any of certain wild canids of the genus Canis, native to the tropical Old World and smaller than a wolf.
A person who performs menial/routine tasks, a dogsbody.
A person who behaves in an opportunistic way; especially a base collaborator.
A jack (the playing card).

To practise witchcraft.
To bewitch.
To dowse for water.

To quickly lower (the head) in order to prevent it from being struck by something.

To go under the surface of water and immediately reappear; to plunge one's head into water or other liquid.
To bow.
To evade doing something.
To lower the volume of (a sound) so that other sounds in the mix can be heard more clearly.
To enter a place for a short moment.

A hooded jacket with a front pocket, usually of fleece material, a kangaroo jacket.

A character class focusing on stealthy conduct.

(by extension) Large, destructive and unpredictable.
(by extension) Deceitful, unprincipled.
Mischievous, unpredictable.
A container made of rigid material, often with a handle, used to carry liquids or small items.

Part of a piece of machinery that resembles a bucket (container).
An old vehicle that is not in good working order.
The basket.

definitions.definition: A field goal.

definitions.definition: To rain heavily.

definitions.definition: To travel very quickly.

definitions.definition: To categorize (data) by splitting it into buckets, or groups of related items.
To ride (a horse) hard or mercilessly.
To make, or cause to make (the recovery), with a certain hurried or unskillful forward swing of the body.
The state of being brothers or a brother.
An association for any purpose, such as a society of monks; a fraternity.
The whole body of persons engaged in the same business, especially those of the same profession

definitions.definition: People, or (poetically) things, of the same kind.
To enjoy, be pleased by; favor; be in favor of.

definitions.definition: To please.
To derive pleasure of, by or with someone or something.
To prefer and maintain (an action) as a regular habit or activity.

definitions.definition: To have an appearance or expression; to look; to seem to be (in a specified condition).
To come near; to avoid with difficulty; to escape narrowly.

definitions.definition: To find attractive; to prefer the company of; to have mild romantic feelings for.
definitions.example: I really like Sandra but don't know how to tell her.

definitions.definition: To liken; to compare.
To show support for, or approval of, something posted on the Internet by marking it with a vote.
definitions.example: I can't stand Bloggs' tomato ketchup, but I liked it on Facebook so I could enter a competition.

definitions.definition: (with 'would' and in certain other phrases) To want, desire. See also would like.
definitions.example: We could go to the museum if you like.

definitions.definition: To be likely.
Having or showing pluck, courage or spirit in trying circumstances.
A piece of clothing worn on the upper body outside a shirt or blouse, often waist length to thigh length.
A piece of a person's suit, beside trousers and, sometimes, waistcoat; coat (US)
A protective or insulating cover for an object (e.g. a book, hot water tank, bullet.)
A police record.
In ordnance, a strengthening band surrounding and reinforcing the tube in which the charge is fired.
The tough outer skin of a baked potato.

To enclose with, or as if with, a wall or walls.

To well, as water; spring.
To make a wall knot on the end of (a rope).

A migratory route used by birds between breeding areas.
The event of setting (someone or something) free (e.g. hostages, slaves, prisoners, caged animals, hooked or stuck mechanisms).
The distribution of an initial or new and upgraded version of a computer software product; the distribution can be either public or private.
Anything recently released or made available (as for sale).

definitions.definition: That which is released, untied or let go.

The process by which a chemical substance is set free.

A device adapted to hold or release a device or mechanism as required.
